title: Material Studio: Luxury Lounge Configurator
description: The user operates an architect's material studio where a ShaderBall2 serves as a live material preview orb on a pedestal beside a GlamVelvetSofa in a minimalist showroom. Users select wall colors, adjust ambient and accent lighting, reposition the sofa along a floor grid, rotate the shader ball to inspect material reflections, toggle between day and night environment presets, scale the sofa for spatial planning, and confirm the final configuration which triggers a cinematic camera flyaround.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a luxury lounge material configurator. Load ShaderBall2.glb and place it on a cylindrical pedestal (radius 0.3, height 1.0) at position (-2, 1, 0). Load GlamVelvetSofa.glb and place it at (1.5, 0, 0) on a reflective floor plane (20x20, MeshStandardMaterial with roughness 0.15, metalness 0.8, color #1a1a1a). Add a subtle grid helper beneath the floor for spatial reference. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0, 3, 7) looking at origin with OrbitControls (damping enabled, min/max distance 3-15). Lighting: one DirectionalLight (intensity 1.5, casting shadows) from (5, 8, 3), one RectAreaLight (warm white, 4x2) behind the sofa simulating a window, and a SpotLight focused on the shader ball. Create an HTML overlay panel with these interactions: (1) A color picker labeled 'Wall Color' that changes a large back-wall plane's color (default #e8e0d4). (2) A slider 'Ambient Intensity' (0.0-2.0) controlling an AmbientLight. (3) A slider 'Accent Spotlight' (0.0-3.0) controlling the SpotLight intensity on the shader ball. (4) Left/Right arrow buttons 'Move Sofa' that translate the sofa ±0.5 along X axis, clamped to -3..5. (5) A 'Rotate Preview' button that animates the shader ball spinning 360 degrees over 2 seconds using requestAnimationFrame. (6) A toggle button 'Day/Night' that switches between a light environment (background #c8d8e8, directional light intensity 1.5) and dark environment (background #0a0a1a, directional light intensity 0.3, spotlight intensity doubled). (7) A scale slider (0.5-1.5) that uniformly scales the sofa. (8) A 'Confirm Design' button that disables all controls and triggers a smooth 5-second camera orbit animation (rotating 360 degrees around the scene center at radius 8, height 4) before returning to the original position. Add shadows on all meshes. Style the panel with a dark semi-transparent background, white text, and rounded corners positioned at top-right.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  wallColor: string,         // current hex color of the back wall plane
  ambientIntensity: number,         // intensity of the ambient light (0.0-2.0)
  accentSpotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the spotlight on shader ball (0.0-3.0)
  sofaPositionX: number,         // X-axis position of the sofa in world space
  shaderBallRotationY: number,         // Y-axis rotation of the shader ball in radians
  directionalLightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the main directional light
  floorReflectivity: number,         // metalness value of the reflective floor (0.0-1.0)
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to origin
  sofaMoveCount: number,         // total number of times sofa has been repositioned
  spotlightAngle: number,         // cone angle of the spotlight in radians
  isShaderBallVisible: boolean,         // whether shader ball is currently visible in scene
  pedestalHeight: number,         // height of the cylindrical pedestal under shader ball
}
